evidence: "I think the other reviewer... gave an excellent review. Although this is a magnificent work..."
"Mozart is the real winner here. Many consider DG the finest opera ever written; I cannot make a good argument against that belief."
"Mozart uses many comedic devises to point out many serious shortcomings in 18th century society."
"The story is sort of complex, off the wall. Think of a 'Class B' 'Lucia'..."
"The story is pretty thin and tawdry and the music sounds like Puccini, but has perhaps two or three really good arias."
"The last act is kind of weak. It has some fine music, but the massacre at the conclusion just does not seem to work very well."
"The sound and videography were also excellent;"
"The audio is 1990 non dts High Def or 5.1 surround and the video is a little grainy non Blu Ray."
"I purchased the Barenboim- La Scala set as the reviews seemed to be pretty good..."
"The battle scenes are unbelievably realistic...very scary..."
"The stage direction, camera work, lighting scenery, costumes and videography, audio are all first rate."
"The final scene's intensity will make the hairs on the back of your neck stand up."

summary: The user has a strong preference for opera and complex narratives, appreciating high production quality and emotional depth in performances. They tend to favor slower pacing, indicating a preference for character-driven stories, while showing some skepticism towards modern interpretations and brand loyalty.
